key_insight: Danus scales research-level mathematical reasoning by coordinating multiple proof-search agents through a shared fact graph that stores verified claims, proofs, and dependencies.
engineering_relevance: The system offers a practical multi-agent orchestration pattern for long-horizon theorem proving, combining planning, parallel search, verification, and memory management into a reusable workflow.
method: Main agent plans, summarizes progress, and redirects search.
Multiple worker agents explore proof ideas in parallel.
A stateless verifier admits only validated claims into the shared fact graph.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports six research-level case studies spanning algebraic geometry, singularity theory, and combinatorics, showing the approach can support long, detailed proofs.
watch_item: Performance may depend heavily on verifier reliability and on how well the fact graph stays consistent as proofs grow and workers explore many branches.
paper_title: Danus: Orchestrating Mathematical Reasoning Agents with Fact-Graph Memory
